Title: Successful User Registration with Valid Data
Preconditions: User is on the registration page of the TrekMate app.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., user@example.com), valid password (e.g., "Password123!"), valid name (e.g., "John Doe"), valid date of birth (e.g., 01/01/1990), valid phone number (e.g., +393123456789)
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to the registration page.
3. Enter valid email, password, name, date of birth, and phone number.
4. Click the "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is successfully created, and a confirmation message is displayed. User is redirected to the login page.

Title: User Registration with Invalid Email Format
Preconditions: User is on the registration page of the TrekMate app.
Test Data: Invalid email (e.g., user@example), valid password (e.g., "Password123!"), valid name (e.g., "John Doe"), valid date of birth (e.g., 01/01/1990), valid phone number (e.g., +393123456789)
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to the registration page.
3. Enter invalid email, valid password, name, date of birth, and phone number.
4. Click the "Register" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed indicating the email format is invalid. User remains on the registration page.

Title: Successful Profile Update with Valid Data
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the profile update page.
Test Data: Valid updated name (e.g., "John Smith"), valid updated phone number (e.g., +393987654321), valid updated date of birth (e.g., 02/02/1990)
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to the profile page.
3. Click on the "Update Profile" option.
4. Enter valid updated name, phone number, and date of birth.
5. Click the "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result: Profile is updated successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Profile Update with Invalid Phone Number Format
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the profile update page.
Test Data: Valid updated name (e.g., "John Smith"), invalid updated phone number (e.g., 1234567890), valid updated date of birth (e.g., 02/02/1990)
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to the profile page.
3. Click on the "Update Profile" option.
4. Enter valid name, invalid phone number, and valid date of birth.
5. Click the "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed indicating the phone number format is invalid. User remains on the profile update page.

Title: Successful Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User is on the login page of the TrekMate app. A valid user account exists.
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to the login page.
3. Enter valid email and password.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in, and the app redirects to the home page.

Title: Failed Login with Invalid Password
Preconditions: User is on the login page of the TrekMate app. A valid user account exists.
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to the login page.
3. Enter valid email and invalid password.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed indicating invalid credentials. User remains on the login page.

Title: Successful Route Selection from Available Routes
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the route selection page. At least one route is available.
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to the route selection page.
3. Browse and select a route from the available list.
4. Click the "Start Trek" button.
Expected Result: Selected route details are displayed, and the user is redirected to the trek preparation page.

Title: Real-Time Position Tracking During Trek
Preconditions: User is logged in, has selected a route, and has started the trek. GPS is enabled on the device.
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to the trek page and start the trek.
3. Observe the real-time position updates on the map.
4. Move to a new location while the trek is in progress.
Expected Result: The app continuously updates the user's position on the map in real-time. The trek progress is accurately reflected.

Title: Position Verification with GPS Disabled
Preconditions: User is logged in, has selected a route, and has started the trek. GPS is disabled on the device.
Test Steps:
1. Open the TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to the trek page and start the trek.
3. Observe the position updates on the map.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed indicating that GPS is required to track the position. The trek cannot start or continue without GPS enabled.

Title: Successful User Registration

Test Data: Valid email (e.g., user@example.com), strong password (e.g., Passw0rd!), full name (e.g., John Doe), date of birth (e.g., 01/01/1990), and valid phone number (e.g., +393123456789)
Test Steps:

2. Navigate to the registration screen.
3. Enter valid email, password, full name, date of birth, and phone number.
4. Confirm the entered data.
5. Tap the "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully, and a confirmation message appears. User is redirected to the login screen or home screen.

Title: User Registration with Existing Email

4. User attaches optional photos or media if available.
5. User submits the report.
Expected Result: Report is successfully submitted, and a confirmation message appears. Admin receives the report in their dashboard for review.

Title: First-time user account creation

Expected Result: The system creates a new account, stores a unique user identifier, and redirects the user to the login screen or app home.

Expected Result: The user is redirected to the profile page displaying personal details (name, email, profile photo, preferences, activity history, and settings).

Test Steps:

2. Modify one or more fields (e.g., email, name).

2. Modify one or more fields.

Title: Selecting a route and displaying weather conditions
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, and the app has access to location and weather services.
Test Data: Valid start and end points on the map
Test Steps:
1. Start tracing a route on the map by selecting start and end points.
2. Allow the system to calculate the route.
3. Wait for the system to fetch and display weather conditions along the route.
Expected Result: The route is displayed on the map with weather information for each segment.

Title: Handling unavailability of weather service
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, and the app has access to location services.
Test Steps:
1. Start tracing a route on the map.
2. Simulate a failure in the weather service response.
Expected Result: The system notifies the user about the unavailability of weather data.

Title: Handling route calculation failure
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, and the app has access to location services.
Test Data: Invalid start or end points (e.g., same location)
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to select a route with invalid or identical start/end points.
2. Confirm the system’s response.
Expected Result: The system prompts the user to re-enter the route points.

Title: Viewing saved trek details
Preconditions: User has completed and saved at least one trek.
Test Steps:
1. Select the "Visualizza Dettagli Percorso" (View Route Details) option.
2. Choose a saved trek from the list.
Expected Result: The system displays detailed trek information (map, duration, distance, altitude changes, and statistics) in a navigable format.

Title: Handling no saved treks
Preconditions: User has not saved any treks.
Test Steps: 1. Select the "Visualizza Dettagli Percorso" (View Route Details) option.
Expected Result: The system displays a message informing the user that no treks are saved and guides them on how to save a trek.

Title: Fetch and Display Weather Information Based on Current User Location
Preconditions:
User is logged in and has granted location permissions to the app.
App has active GPS and internet connection.
User has started a trek/excursion.
Test Data:
User location: Latitude X, Longitude Y (valid coordinates).
Weather service response: JSON with temperature, precipitation, wind speed, and weather phenomena (e.g., rain, snow).
Test Steps:
1. User starts a trek/excursion in the app.
2. System automatically acquires user’s current location (Latitude X, Longitude Y).
3. System requests weather data for the acquired location from the weather service.
4. System processes and formats the weather data (e.g., highlights rain warning, wind speed).
5. System displays weather details (temperature, precipitation, wind) and provides contextual advice (e.g., "Bring a raincoat").
6. System monitors for significant weather changes during the trek.
Expected Result:
Weather data for Latitude X, Longitude Y is fetched and displayed correctly.
Relevant weather phenomena (e.g., rain, strong wind) are highlighted with appropriate advice.
If significant weather change occurs (e.g., sudden rain), user is notified immediately.
--

Title: Display Generic Weather Advice When Weather Service is Unavailable
Preconditions:
User is logged in and has started a trek/excursion.
App has GPS but no internet connection (weather service unavailable).
Test Data:
User location: Latitude X, Longitude Y.
App’s local database contains generic weather advice for the region/season (e.g., "Mountain areas are cold in winter").
Test Steps:
1. User starts a trek/excursion.
2. System detects no internet connection (weather service unavailable).
3. System retrieves generic weather advice for Latitude X, Longitude Y from local data.
4. System displays the generic advice (e.g., "Typical summer conditions: warm days, possible afternoon thunderstorms").
Expected Result:
Generic weather advice for the region/season is displayed instead of real-time data.
No error message is shown; advice is presented in a user-friendly format.
--

Title: Verify and Fetch Weather for Manually Selected Position on Map
Preconditions:
User is logged in and registered.
App has access to mapping service and weather service.
User is on the main map screen.
Test Data:
Selected position: Latitude A, Longitude B (a known hiking trail location).
Weather service response: JSON with weather data for Latitude A, Longitude B (e.g., "Partly cloudy, 15°C").
Test Steps:
1. User taps on a specific location (Latitude A, Longitude B) on the map.
2. System shows an indicator (e.g., pin) at the selected position.
3. System requests and displays weather conditions for Latitude A, Longitude B.
4. User verifies the position and weather data.
Expected Result:
Indicator is displayed at Latitude A, Longitude B.
Weather data for the selected position is fetched and displayed correctly.
User can confirm the position and weather details are accurate.
--

Title: Filter Past Workouts by Date and Type
Preconditions:
User is logged in.
User has multiple workouts recorded (e.g., 3 hikes, 2 runs).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Health" section.
2. User applies filter: Type = "Hike".
3. User applies filter: Date range = "2023-10-01 to 2023-10-03".
Expected Result:
Only Hike 1 (2023-10-01) and Hike 2 (2023-10-03) are displayed in the list.
--

Title: Delete a Past Workout
Preconditions:
User is logged in.
User has at least one recorded workout (e.g., Workout 1: 2023-10-01).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Health" section.
2. User selects Workout 1 (2023-10-01).
3. User clicks the "Delete" option for the workout.
4. System prompts for confirmation: "Are you sure you want to delete this workout?"
5. User confirms deletion.
Expected Result:
Workout 1 is removed from the list of past workouts.
No error occurs during deletion.
--

Title: Start and Stop Training Session Recording
Preconditions:
User is logged in.
User has granted location and sensor permissions (steps, distance).
Test Data: Initial state: No active training session.
Test Steps:
1. User taps the "Start Training" button.
2. System starts timer and begins recording steps, distance, and calories.
3. User walks/hikes for 10 minutes (simulated or real).
4. User taps the "Stop Training" button.
5. System stops timer and saves the session data.
Expected Result:
Timer starts at Step 1 and stops at Step 4.
Recorded data includes: duration (10 mins), steps (>500), distance (>0.5 km), calories (>50).
Saved session appears in the "Health" section with the recorded statistics.
--

Title: View Real-Time Statistics During Training
Preconditions:
User is logged in.
Training session is in progress (timer running).
Test Data: Real-time data during session: Steps = 800, Distance = 1.2 km, Calories = 120, Duration = 15 mins.
Test Steps:
1. User starts a training session.
2. User taps the "Statistics" button during the session.
Expected Result:
Pop-up or overlay displays real-time values:
Steps: 800
Distance: 1.2 km
Calories: 120
Duration: 15 mins
Values update dynamically as the session progresses.
--

Title: Record and Monitor Training Statistics
Preconditions: User is logged in and has started a training session
Test Data: Training session data (e.g., duration: 30 mins, distance: 5 km, steps: 6000, calories: 250)
Test Steps:

Title: Verify accuracy of weather information and event alerts
Preconditions: User has logged in and accessed the weather or event section.
Test Data: Predefined weather data (e.g., temperature, precipitation) and event alerts (e.g., trail closures, weather warnings).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the weather section and display current conditions.
2. Compare displayed weather data with a trusted external source (e.g., official meteorological service).
3. Navigate to the event alerts section and verify displayed alerts.
4. Cross-check alerts with official sources or predefined test data.
Expected Result: Displayed weather data and event alerts must match the trusted source exactly. No discrepancies or outdated information should be present.

Title: Validate user interaction reliability
Preconditions: User is logged in and interacting with the app.
Test Data: Valid and invalid inputs (e.g., incorrect date formats, non-existent trail names).
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to submit a valid user action (e.g., saving a route, updating preferences).
2. Attempt to submit an invalid input (e.g., entering a non-existent trail name).
3. Observe system behavior for both valid and invalid inputs.
Expected Result: Valid inputs must be processed successfully without errors. Invalid inputs must trigger appropriate error messages or prompts for correction without crashing the app.

Title: Test system resilience to malfunctions or erroneous inputs
Preconditions: User is logged in and has active sessions.
Test Data: Simulated malfunctions (e.g., network interruption, forced app closure) and erroneous inputs (e.g., rapid successive inputs, incorrect data formats).
Test Steps:
1. Simulate a network interruption while the app is fetching weather data.
2. Reconnect and verify if the app recovers gracefully (e.g., retry mechanism, cached data fallback).
3. Enter rapid successive inputs (e.g., repeatedly tapping a button).
4. Enter incorrect data formats (e.g., invalid email, phone number).
Expected Result: The app must continue to operate satisfactorily after network recovery, using cached data if necessary. Rapid inputs must be handled without freezing or crashing. Incorrect data formats must trigger validation errors without disrupting other functionalities.

Title: Verify data consistency during profile updates
Preconditions: User has an existing profile with predefined data (e.g., name, email, preferences).
Test Data: Modified profile data (e.g., updated email, new preferences).
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the app and navigate to the profile section.
2. Update one or more profile fields (e.g., email, phone number, preferred language).
3. Log out and log back in.
4. Verify that the updated data is reflected in the profile section.
5. Check backend database (if accessible) to confirm the changes are saved correctly.
Expected Result: All updated profile fields must be saved and reflected accurately in both the app interface and backend. No discrepancies between displayed data and backend data should exist.

Title: Test cross-platform functionality on different devices/OS
Preconditions: App is installed on multiple devices with different OS versions (e.g., iOS 15/16, Android 12/13).
Test Data: Standard user interactions (e.g., navigating menus, viewing weather, updating profile).
Test Steps:
1. Perform a set of standard interactions (e.g., open app, view weather, update profile) on an iOS device.
2. Repeat the same interactions on an Android device.
3. Compare the user experience, interface rendering, and functionality across devices.
4. Test on devices with varying screen sizes (e.g., smartphone, tablet).
Expected Result: The app must function correctly and provide a consistent user experience across all tested devices and OS versions. No crashes, rendering issues, or missing functionalities should occur.

Title: Measure resource usage efficiency
Preconditions: App is running on a device with monitoring tools (e.g., Android Profiler, Xcode Instruments).
Test Data: Multiple concurrent user actions (e.g., loading weather data, displaying maps, updating profile).
Test Steps:
1. Open the app and perform multiple concurrent actions (e.g., load weather data while updating profile).
2. Monitor CPU, memory, and battery usage via device tools.
3. Measure response times for critical actions (e.g., loading a map, submitting a form).
4. Simulate high network traffic conditions.
Expected Result: CPU and memory usage must remain within acceptable limits (e.g., <70% CPU, <50% RAM on average devices). Response times must be under 2 seconds for standard actions and under 5 seconds for complex operations. Battery drain must be minimal during normal usage.

Title: Validate GDPR compliance and response times
Preconditions: User has submitted a data request (e.g., profile update, data deletion).
Test Data: Sensitive user data (e.g., email, phone number) and deletion requests.
Test Steps:
1. Submit a profile update request containing sensitive data.
2. Measure the time taken to process and save the request.
3. Submit a data deletion request (e.g., delete account or specific data fields).
4. Measure the time taken to process and confirm deletion.
5. Verify that all personal data is permanently removed from the system (if applicable).
Expected Result: The system must process and save/update data within 2 seconds. Deletion requests must be completed within 5 seconds. All GDPR requirements (e.g., data minimization, right to erasure) must be met, and no residual data should remain after deletion.

Title: Verify Intuitive Navigation Flow
Preconditions: Application installed and launched on a device
Test Steps:
1. Open the application for the first time.
2. Attempt to locate the main menu options (e.g., "Map," "Profile," "Settings") without prior guidance.
3. Navigate to a specific feature (e.g., "Weather Forecast") using only the interface elements.
4. Return to the home screen using the back button or equivalent.
Expected Result: The user can locate and access all primary features within 3 attempts without confusion or need for external help.

Title: Verify Continuous Internet Connectivity for Core Services
Preconditions: Device with active internet connection (Wi-Fi or mobile data)
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application with an active internet connection.
2. Attempt to access mapping services (e.g., load a map or search for a location).
3. Attempt to fetch weather information for the current location.
4. Attempt to authenticate as a registered user.
5. Simulate a loss of internet connection (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi/mobile data).
6. Attempt to perform any of the actions in steps 2–4 while offline.
Expected Result: Steps 1–4 succeed with no errors. Steps 5–6 fail gracefully with an appropriate error message (e.g., "No internet connection. Please check your settings").

Title: Verify GPS Data Accuracy for Navigation
Preconditions: Device with GPS enabled and outdoors in an open area
Test Data: Known geographic coordinates (e.g., latitude/longitude of a landmark)
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application and grant GPS permissions if prompted.
2. Allow the application to access GPS data.
3. Verify the displayed current location matches the actual device location within a 50-meter radius.
4. Move to a new location (e.g., walk 100 meters) and confirm the application updates the position in real-time.
5. Compare the GPS-derived location with a secondary source (e.g., Google Maps).
Expected Result: The application accurately reflects the device’s location in real-time with minimal delay and within the acceptable error margin.

Title: Verify Server-Side User Authentication
Preconditions: Registered user account with valid credentials
Test Data: Valid username/password, invalid username/password, expired session token
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to log in with valid credentials.
2. Attempt to log in with invalid credentials (e.g., wrong password).
3. After successful login, log out and attempt to access a protected feature (e.g., "My Trips").
4. Simulate an expired session by clearing cookies/cache or using a session token from another device.
5. Attempt to access a protected feature while logged out or with an expired session.
Expected Result: Step 1 succeeds with access granted. Steps 2 and 5 fail with an error message (e.g., "Invalid credentials" or "Session expired. Please log in again"). Step 3 redirects to the login screen or denies access.

Title: Verify user interaction response time
Preconditions: Application is installed and running on a supported device.
Test Steps:
1. Open the application.
2. Perform any user interaction (e.g., tap a button, navigate to a screen).
3. Measure the time taken for the system to respond.
Expected Result: The system responds within 2 seconds for all user interactions.

Title: Verify concurrent user handling
Preconditions: Application is installed and running on a test environment.
Test Steps:
1. Simulate 1000 users accessing the application simultaneously.
2. Monitor system stability and performance.
Expected Result: The application handles 1000 concurrent users without crashes or significant degradation in performance.

Title: Verify UI intuitiveness
Preconditions: Application is installed on a test device.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application for the first time.
2. Attempt to complete a basic task (e.g., register, log in, or navigate to profile) without prior guidance.
Expected Result: The user interface is intuitive and allows the user to complete the task with minimal effort.

Title: Verify data accuracy
Preconditions: Application is installed and logged in with a test account.
Test Data: Predefined test data with known values (e.g., distance, elevation).
Test Steps:
1. Record a test activity with predefined data.
2. Compare the displayed data (e.g., distance, elevation) with the recorded values.
Expected Result: The displayed data matches the recorded values with a maximum error margin of 1%.

Title: Verify user authentication
Preconditions: Application is installed and running.
Test Data: Valid and invalid credentials for a test account.
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to log in with valid credentials.
2. Attempt to log in with invalid credentials.
Expected Result: The system authenticates valid credentials successfully and rejects invalid credentials.

Title: Verify Android compatibility
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on a device running Android 8.1.
2. Install the application on a device running a newer Android version (e.g., Android 10, 11, or 12).
3. Perform basic operations (e.g., registration, login, navigation).
Expected Result: The application is compatible and functions correctly on Android 8.1 and later versions.

Title: Verify system resource optimization
Preconditions: Application is installed on a test device.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application and monitor CPU, memory, and battery usage.
2. Perform multiple operations (e.g., navigation, data input) simultaneously.
Expected Result: The application optimizes system resources and does not cause excessive CPU, memory, or battery drain.

Title: Verify new account creation on first launch
Preconditions: Application is downloaded and installed on a device.
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application for the first time.
2. Select the option to create a new account.
3. Fill in the registration form with valid user details.
4. Submit the form.
Expected Result: The account is created successfully, and the user can navigate within the application with full access to functionalities.

Title: Verify profile visualization
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, and has an active internet connection.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the profile section of the application.
2. Verify that all user information is displayed correctly.
Expected Result: The user’s profile information is displayed accurately, and navigation between profile sections is possible.

Title: Verify profile information update
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, and has an active profile.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the profile section.
2. Select the option to update personal information.
3. Modify one or more fields (e.g., name, email).
4. Save the changes.
Expected Result: The updated information is saved and reflected in the user’s profile.

Title: Verify user login functionality
Preconditions: Application is installed, and a test account is registered.
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to log in with valid credentials.
2. Attempt to log in with invalid credentials.
Expected Result: The system grants access with valid credentials and denies access with invalid credentials.

Title: Select and View Weather Information for a Route
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, and has access to mapping and weather services.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the mapping feature.
3. User selects a predefined route (e.g., "Alpine Trail").
4. User requests weather information for the selected route.
Expected Result: The selected route and associated weather information (e.g., temperature, precipitation, wind speed) are displayed to the user.

Title: View Detailed Information of Saved Routes
Preconditions: User has completed a hike, saved the route in the app, and is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the "Saved Routes" section.
3. User selects a previously saved route (e.g., "Forest Loop").
Expected Result: Detailed information about the selected route (e.g., distance, elevation, duration, weather conditions at the time) is displayed.

Title: Receive Real-Time Weather Updates During a Hike
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, and has started a hike.
Test Data: Current location data and active hike session.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User starts a hike session.
3. User navigates to the weather monitoring feature.
4. User verifies real-time weather updates for the current location.
Expected Result: User receives accurate and timely weather information (e.g., temperature, precipitation, wind alerts) relevant to the hike.

Title: Verify Weather Information for a Specific Location
Preconditions: User is registered, logged in, app has access to user’s current location, and is connected to mapping and weather services.
Test Data: A specific GPS coordinate (e.g., latitude 45.1234, longitude 9.5678).
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User selects a specific location on the map (e.g., latitude 45.1234, longitude 9.5678).
3. User requests weather information for the selected location.
Expected Result: Weather information (e.g., temperature, precipitation, wind speed) for the selected location is displayed.

Title: View Past Workouts Chronologically
Preconditions: User has logged in and has past workouts recorded.
Test Data: Multiple past workouts (e.g., 3 hikes completed in the last month).
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the "Workout History" section.
3. User views the list of past workouts.
Expected Result: Past workouts are displayed in chronological order (newest first or oldest first, as per design).

Title: View Detailed Information of a Specific Past Workout
Preconditions: User has logged in and has past workouts recorded.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User navigates to the "Workout History" section.
3. User selects a specific past workout (e.g., "Mountain Climb - 2023-10-15").
Expected Result: Detailed information about the selected workout (e.g., duration, distance, elevation gain, calories burned) is displayed.

Title: View Past Workouts Offline
Preconditions: User has logged in, has past workouts recorded, and is in offline mode.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User disconnects from the internet (offline mode).
3. User navigates to the "Workout History" section.
Expected Result: Past workouts are displayed without requiring an internet connection.

Title: Start and Stop Workout Recording
Preconditions: User has logged in and selected the option to start workout recording.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User selects the "Start Workout" option.
3. User waits for 30 seconds.
4. User selects the "Stop Workout" option.
Expected Result: Workout recording starts and stops successfully, and the interface reflects the active/inactive state.

Title: View Real-Time Workout Statistics
Preconditions: User has logged in and started a workout recording.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User starts a workout recording.
3. User waits for 1 minute.
4. User views the real-time statistics displayed on the screen.
Expected Result: Real-time statistics (duration, steps, distance, calories burned) are displayed and updated continuously during the workout.

Title: Save Workout Statistics Permanently
Preconditions: User has logged in, started a workout, and stopped the recording.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in to the application.
2. User starts a workout recording.
3. User waits for 20 minutes.
4. User stops the workout recording.
5. User logs out and logs back in.
6. User navigates to the "Workout History" section.
Expected Result: The completed workout statistics are saved and associated with the user’s profile, and are visible in the workout history.

Title: Verify GPS Module Functionality
Preconditions: Device has Android 8.1+ and GPS module enabled
Test Steps:
1. Open TrekMate app.
2. Navigate to a trek route selection screen.
3. Ensure GPS signal is active (indicated by a GPS icon or status bar).
4. Start tracking a trek route.
Expected Result: App accurately tracks the user’s location in real-time with minimal latency.

Title: Verify Offline Map Functionality
Preconditions: Device has downloaded offline maps for a specific region
Test Steps:
1. Disable mobile data/Wi-Fi on the device.
2. Open TrekMate and select a trek route in the pre-downloaded offline region.
3. Navigate to the map view.
4. Follow the route on the map.
Expected Result: App displays the route and map details without requiring an internet connection.

Title: Verify Emergency Call Integration
Preconditions: Device has network connectivity and emergency contacts configured
Test Steps:
1. Open TrekMate and select a trek route.
2. Simulate an emergency by triggering the emergency call button (if available).
3. Verify the call connects to the predefined emergency number.
Expected Result: Call is successfully initiated to the emergency contact without delays.

Title: Verify Trail Status Display
Preconditions: App has updated trail status data from CAI/Forestale
Test Data: Trail with "last maintenance date," "closed sections," or "hazard warnings"
Test Steps:
1. Open TrekMate and select a trek route with known trail status updates.
2. Navigate to the trail details section.
3. Check for displayed warnings (e.g., "last maintenance: 2023-10-01," "section closed due to landslide").
Expected Result: App displays accurate and up-to-date trail status information, including maintenance dates and hazards.

Title: Verify User-Generated Trail Issue Reporting
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a reported trail issue
Test Data: Reported issue (e.g., "broken bridge on route X")
Test Steps:
1. Open TrekMate and navigate to the trail reporting section.
2. Select a route and submit a new issue (e.g., "obstacle on path").
3. Verify the issue is submitted successfully.
Expected Result: Report is saved in the system and visible in the admin/CAI dashboard for review.

Title: Verify Trek Difficulty Level Display
Preconditions: Trek route has predefined difficulty levels (e.g., easy, medium, hard)
Test Steps:
1. Open TrekMate and select a trek route.
2. Navigate to the route details screen.
3. Check the displayed difficulty level.
Expected Result: App correctly displays the predefined difficulty level (e.g., "medium") for the selected route.

Title: Verify Personal Objectives Unlocking Based on Distance
Preconditions: User has completed a trek covering a predefined distance (e.g., 5 km)
Test Data: Trek route of 5 km with an objective set at "unlock badge after 5 km"
Test Steps:
1. Complete a 5 km trek using TrekMate.
2. Navigate to the user profile or achievements section.
3. Check for unlocked objectives or badges.
Expected Result: App unlocks the predefined objective/badge after completing the 5 km trek.

Title: Verify App Installation on Minimum Device Specifications
Preconditions: Device meets minimum requirements (Android 8.1, 1 GB RAM, 300 MB storage)
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to install TrekMate on a device with Android 8.1, 1 GB RAM, and 300 MB free storage.
2. Open the app and navigate to the main screen.
3. Perform basic actions (e.g., select a route).
Expected Result: App installs successfully, launches without crashes, and functions as expected on minimum specifications.

Title: Verify App Behavior in Low/No Network Conditions
Preconditions: Device has offline maps downloaded
Test Steps:
1. Disable mobile data/Wi-Fi on the device.
2. Open TrekMate and attempt to access real-time features (e.g., live trail updates, emergency calls).
3. Switch back to online mode and retry.
Expected Result: App disables network-dependent features in offline mode and re-enables them upon reconnecting to the network. Offline maps and pre-downloaded data remain accessible.
